title: CART (Classification and Regression Trees) Segmentation
description: This codebook demonstrates the backend code used to perform CART-based segmentation using decision trees with Gini impurity criterion.

sections.title: 1. Import Required Libraries
sections.content:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeClassifier, DecisionTreeRegressor
from sklearn.preprocessing import LabelEncoder, StandardScaler
from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn import tree

sections.title: 2. Load and Prepare Dataset
sections.content:
# Load your dataset
df = pd.read_csv('your_dataset.csv')

# Define target variable
target_variable = 'target_column'

# Define segmentation variables
segmentation_variables = ['var1', 'var2', 'var3']

print(f"Dataset shape: {df.shape}")
print(f"Target variable: {target_variable}")
print(f"Segmentation variables: {segmentation_variables}")

sections.title: 3. Data Preprocessing
sections.content:
# Separate features and target
X = df[segmentation_variables]
y = df[target_variable]

# Handle missing values
imputer = SimpleImputer(strategy='mean')
X_imputed = imputer.fit_transform(X)

# Convert back to DataFrame to keep column names
X_clean = pd.DataFrame(X_imputed, columns=segmentation_variables)

print(f"Cleaned data shape: {X_clean.shape}")
print(f"Missing values: {X_clean.isnull().sum().sum()}")

sections.title: 4. Encode Categorical Variables
sections.content:
# Identify categorical columns
categorical_cols = X_clean.select_dtypes(include=['object']).columns

# Label encode categorical variables
label_encoders = {}
for col in categorical_cols:
    le = LabelEncoder()
    X_clean[col] = le.fit_transform(X_clean[col].astype(str))
    label_encoders[col] = le

print(f"Encoded {len(categorical_cols)} categorical variables")

sections.title: 5. Initialize CART Model
sections.content:
# Determine if classification or regression based on target
problem_type = 'classification' if y.nunique() <= 20 else 'regression'

# Set segmentation parameters
max_depth = 4
min_samples_leaf = 25
max_leaf_nodes = 5

# Initialize decision tree with Gini criterion
if problem_type == 'classification':
    model = DecisionTreeClassifier(
        criterion='gini',              # CART uses Gini impurity
        max_depth=max_depth,
        min_samples_leaf=min_samples_leaf,
        max_leaf_nodes=max_leaf_nodes,
        random_state=42
    )
else:
    model = DecisionTreeRegressor(
        criterion='squared_error',     # For regression
        max_depth=max_depth,
        min_samples_leaf=min_samples_leaf,
        max_leaf_nodes=max_leaf_nodes,
        random_state=42
    )

print(f"Model initialized: {type(model).__name__}")
print(f"Max depth: {max_depth}")
print(f"Min samples per leaf: {min_samples_leaf}")

sections.title: 6. Train Segmentation Model
sections.content:
# Fit the decision tree to create segments
model.fit(X_clean, y)

print(f"Model trained successfully")
print(f"Tree depth: {model.get_depth()}")
print(f"Number of leaves: {model.get_n_leaves()}")

sections.title: 7. Extract Segment Assignments
sections.content:
# Get segment assignments (leaf node IDs)
segment_ids = model.apply(X_clean)

# Create segment mapping
unique_segments = np.unique(segment_ids)
segment_mapping = {old_id: new_id for new_id, old_id in enumerate(unique_segments)}

# Remap to consecutive segment numbers (1, 2, 3, ...)
segments = np.array([segment_mapping[sid] + 1 for sid in segment_ids])

# Add segments to dataframe
df['segment'] = segments

print(f"Created {len(unique_segments)} segments")
print(f"Segment distribution:")
print(df['segment'].value_counts().sort_index())

sections.title: 8. Extract Segment Rules
sections.content:
# Extract decision path for each segment
def get_segment_rules(tree_model, feature_names, segment_id):
    tree_ = tree_model.tree_
    feature_name = [
        feature_names[i] if i != -2 else "undefined!"
        for i in tree_.feature
    ]
    
    # Find path to leaf node
    node_indicator = tree_model.decision_path(X_clean)
    leaf_id = tree_model.apply(X_clean)
    
    # Get samples in this segment
    sample_id = np.where(leaf_id == segment_id)[0][0]
    node_index = node_indicator.indices[node_indicator.indptr[sample_id]:
                                       node_indicator.indptr[sample_id + 1]]
    
    rules = []
    for node_id in node_index[:-1]:  # Exclude leaf node
        if X_clean.iloc[sample_id, tree_.feature[node_id]] <= tree_.threshold[node_id]:
            threshold_sign = "<="
        else:
            threshold_sign = ">"
        
        rules.append(f"{feature_name[node_id]} {threshold_sign} {tree_.threshold[node_id]:.2f}")
    
    return rules

# Print rules for each segment
for seg_id in unique_segments:
    rules = get_segment_rules(model, segmentation_variables, seg_id)
    segment_num = segment_mapping[seg_id] + 1
    print(f"\nSegment {segment_num} Rules:")
    for rule in rules:
        print(f"  - {rule}")

sections.title: 9. Segment Performance Metrics
sections.content:
# Calculate metrics for each segment
segment_metrics = []

for seg_num in sorted(df['segment'].unique()):
    seg_data = df[df['segment'] == seg_num]
    
    metrics = {
        'segment': seg_num,
        'size': len(seg_data),
        'proportion': len(seg_data) / len(df),
        'target_mean': seg_data[target_variable].mean(),
        'target_std': seg_data[target_variable].std()
    }
    
    if problem_type == 'classification':
        # Event rate for classification
        metrics['event_rate'] = (seg_data[target_variable] == 1).mean()
    
    segment_metrics.append(metrics)

# Convert to DataFrame
metrics_df = pd.DataFrame(segment_metrics)
print("\nSegment Metrics:")
print(metrics_df)

sections.title: 10. Visualize Decision Tree
sections.content:
# Visualize the decision tree structure
plt.figure(figsize=(20, 10))
tree.plot_tree(
    model,
    feature_names=segmentation_variables,
    class_names=['Class 0', 'Class 1'] if problem_type == 'classification' else None,
    filled=True,
    rounded=True,
    fontsize=10
)
plt.title('CART Segmentation Tree', fontsize=16)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('cart_tree.png', dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
print("Tree visualization saved as 'cart_tree.png'")

sections.title: 11. Segment Profiling - Calculate Distributions
12. Statistical Testing - Chi-Squared Test
sections.content:
# Perform Chi-squared test to verify segments have statistically significant differences
from scipy.stats import chi2_contingency, f_oneway

print("="*60)
print("STATISTICAL TESTING - Chi-Squared Test")
print("="*60)

# For categorical target (classification)
if problem_type == 'classification':
    # Create contingency table: segments vs target
    contingency_table = pd.crosstab(df['segment'], df[target_variable])
    
    print("\nContingency Table (Segments vs Target):")
    print(contingency_table)
    
    # Perform chi-squared test
    chi2_stat, p_value, dof, expected_freq = chi2_contingency(contingency_table)
    
    print("\n" + "-"*60)
    print("Statistical Test Results:")
    print("-"*60)
    print(f"Test Used: Chi-Squared Test")
    print(f"Chi-squared statistic (χ²): {chi2_stat:.4f}")
    print(f"P-value: {p_value:.6f}")
    print(f"Degrees of freedom: {dof}")
    print("-"*60)
    
    # Interpretation with three levels of significance
    if p_value < 0.05:
        print("\n✓ Result: STATISTICALLY SIGNIFICANT (p < 0.05)")
        print("  → Segments have meaningfully different event rates")
        print("  → Good segmentation! Segments are well-differentiated")
    elif p_value < 0.10:
        print("\n⚠ Result: MARGINALLY SIGNIFICANT (0.05 ≤ p < 0.10)")
        print("  → Segments show some differences, but not strongly")
        print("  → Consider: lowering min segment size, increasing max segments,")
        print("    or selecting stronger predictor variables")
    else:
        print("\n✗ Result: NOT SIGNIFICANT (p ≥ 0.10)")
        print("  → Segments do NOT have meaningfully different event rates")
        print("  → Recommendations:")
        print("    1. Use fewer but stronger predictor variables")
        print("    2. Lower minimum segment size (try 500-1000)")
        print("    3. Increase maximum segments (try 8-10)")
        print("    4. Try switching between CART and CHAID methods")

# For continuous target (regression)
else:
    # Perform one-way ANOVA
    segment_groups = [df[df['segment'] == seg][target_variable].values 
                     for seg in sorted(df['segment'].unique())]
    
    f_stat, p_value = f_oneway(*segment_groups)
    
    print("\n" + "-"*60)
    print("Statistical Test Results (ANOVA):")
    print("-"*60)
    print(f"Test Used: One-Way ANOVA")
    print(f"F-statistic: {f_stat:.4f}")
    print(f"P-value: {p_value:.6f}")
    print("-"*60)
    
    if p_value < 0.05:
        print("\n✓ Result: STATISTICALLY SIGNIFICANT (p < 0.05)")
        print("  → Segments have significantly different target values")
    elif p_value < 0.10:
        print("\n⚠ Result: MARGINALLY SIGNIFICANT (0.05 ≤ p < 0.10)")
        print("  → Segments show marginal differences")
    else:
        print("\n✗ Result: NOT SIGNIFICANT (p ≥ 0.10)")
        print("  → Segments do not have significantly different target values")

sections.title: 13. Stability Test - Cross-Validation
sections.content:
# Validate segmentation consistency using cross-validation
from sklearn.model_selection import KFold
from sklearn.metrics import adjusted_rand_score

# Perform k-fold cross-validation
kf = KFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=42)
stability_scores = []

print("\nStability Test (Cross-Validation):")
print("Testing if segments remain consistent across different data splits...\n")

for fold_idx, (train_idx, test_idx) in enumerate(kf.split(X_clean), 1):
    # Train on fold
    X_train_fold = X_clean.iloc[train_idx]
    y_train_fold = y.iloc[train_idx]
    
    X_test_fold = X_clean.iloc[test_idx]
    
    # Train model on this fold
    if problem_type == 'classification':
        fold_model = DecisionTreeClassifier(
            criterion='gini',
            max_depth=max_depth,
            min_samples_leaf=min_samples_leaf,
            max_leaf_nodes=max_leaf_nodes,
            random_state=42
        )
    else:
        fold_model = DecisionTreeRegressor(
            criterion='squared_error',
            max_depth=max_depth,
            min_samples_leaf=min_samples_leaf,
            max_leaf_nodes=max_leaf_nodes,
            random_state=42
        )
    
    fold_model.fit(X_train_fold, y_train_fold)
    
    # Predict segments on test fold
    test_segments_fold = fold_model.apply(X_test_fold)
    
    # Compare with original segments (stability check)
    original_test_segments = segment_ids[test_idx]
    
    # Calculate Adjusted Rand Index (measures similarity between clusterings)
    ari_score = adjusted_rand_score(original_test_segments, test_segments_fold)
    stability_scores.append(ari_score)
    
    print(f"Fold {fold_idx}: ARI = {ari_score:.4f}")

avg_stability = np.mean(stability_scores)
print(f"\nAverage Cross-validation Stability: {avg_stability:.4f}")

if avg_stability > 0.7:
    print("✓ Segmentation is STABLE (score > 0.7)")
    print("  Segments remain consistent across different data splits")
elif avg_stability > 0.5:
    print("⚠ Segmentation is MODERATELY STABLE (0.5 < score <= 0.7)")
    print("  Some variation in segments across splits")
else:
    print("✗ Segmentation is UNSTABLE (score <= 0.5)")
    print("  Segments vary significantly - consider adjusting parameters")

sections.title: 14. Filter Non-viable Segments
sections.content:
# Remove segments that are too small or don't meet minimum requirements
min_segment_size = 25  # Minimum records per segment
min_segment_proportion = 0.02  # Minimum 2% of total data

print("\nFiltering Non-viable Segments:")
print(f"Minimum size requirement: {min_segment_size} records")
print(f"Minimum proportion requirement: {min_segment_proportion:.1%}\n")

viable_segments = []
non_viable_segments = []

for seg_num in sorted(df['segment'].unique()):
    seg_size = len(df[df['segment'] == seg_num])
    seg_proportion = seg_size / len(df)
    
    is_viable = (seg_size >= min_segment_size) and (seg_proportion >= min_segment_proportion)
    
    if is_viable:
        viable_segments.append(seg_num)
        print(f"✓ Segment {seg_num}: {seg_size} records ({seg_proportion:.1%}) - VIABLE")
    else:
        non_viable_segments.append(seg_num)
        reason = []
        if seg_size < min_segment_size:
            reason.append(f"too small ({seg_size} < {min_segment_size})")
        if seg_proportion < min_segment_proportion:
            reason.append(f"proportion too low ({seg_proportion:.1%} < {min_segment_proportion:.1%})")
        print(f"✗ Segment {seg_num}: {seg_size} records ({seg_proportion:.1%}) - NON-VIABLE ({', '.join(reason)})")

print(f"\nViability Results:")
print(f"Viable segments: {len(viable_segments)} out of {len(df['segment'].unique())}")
print(f"Non-viable segments removed: {len(non_viable_segments)}")

if len(non_viable_segments) > 0:
    print(f"\n⚠ Recommendation: Consider recreating segmentation with different parameters")
    print(f"  to reduce the number of non-viable segments.")
else:
    print(f"\n✓ All segments meet minimum size and quality requirements")

# Filter dataframe to keep only viable segments
df_viable = df[df['segment'].isin(viable_segments)].copy()
print(f"\nFiltered dataset: {len(df_viable)} records in {len(viable_segments)} viable segments")
